mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in over 65% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and stable consumer prices indicates robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over 60-day horizons, with a Sharpe ratio below 0.5 in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery phases, with a t-statistic below 1.5 on the coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect economic strength and higher aggregate income, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns, with an out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates beyond 2.5% annually while policy rates remain constrained, markets price in aggressive future tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 70% of periods where the inflation threshold is breached without rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in housing starts and real GDP growth confirms broad-based economic demand, boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined housing and GDP signal yields negative alpha over 20-day horizons, with a cumulative drawdown exceeding 5% in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production coinciding with rising unemployment indicates a supply-side contraction and weakening economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX returns are not significantly negative during supply contractions, with a p-value above 0.10 on the directional test.
